```{r}
library(httk)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Compare free fraction (eta_free) and partitioning across a
# range of FBS concentrations, for each PFAS chemical.
# This directly addresses the reviewer comment about whether/how
# serum supplementation (10% FBS) affects PFAS bioavailability,
# by showing the predicted free fraction at 0%, 1%, and 10% FBS.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

chemicals <- tibble::tibble(
  Chemical = c("PFHxS", "PFNA", "PFOA", "PFOS", "PFDA", "Genx"),
  cas      = c("355-46-4", "375-95-1", "335-67-1", "1763-23-1", "335-76-2", "62037-80-3")
)

# FBS fractions to compare: 0%, 1%, 10%
fbs_levels <- c(0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1)

extract_cfree <- function(obj, chem, fbs) {
  obj %>%
    mutate(
      Chemical = chem,
      FBSf     = fbs
    ) %>%
    select(Chemical, FBSf, eta_free, xplastic, xbsa, xair, xdom, xslip, xcells, xprecip)
}

# Run armitage_eval for every chemical x FBS level combination
results_list <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(chemicals))) {
  for (fbs in fbs_levels) {

    res <- armitage_eval(
      chem.cas       = chemicals$cas[i],
      this.well_number = 96,
      this.FBSf      = fbs,
      this.v_total   = 200,
      this.cell_yield = 200000,
      nomconc.vector = 100,
      this.Tsys      = 37,
      this.cell_pH   = 7.4
    )

    results_list[[paste(chemicals$Chemical[i], fbs, sep = "_")]] <-
      extract_cfree(res, chemicals$Chemical[i], fbs)
  }
}

cfree_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

# Make FBS level a readable factor for plotting
cfree_df <- cfree_df %>%
  mutate(
    FBS_label = factor(
      paste0(FBSf * 100, "% FBS"),
      levels = paste0(sort(unique(fbs_levels)) * 100, "% FBS")
    )
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot 1: Free fraction (eta_free) vs FBS concentration,
# one line/point per chemical -- the key figure for the rebuttal
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
p_free <- ggplot(cfree_df,
                  aes(x = FBS_label, y = eta_free,
                      color = Chemical, group = Chemical)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Serum (FBS) concentration",
    y = "Free fraction (eta_free)",
    color = "Chemical",
    title = "Predicted free fraction of PFAS across FBS concentrations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 16),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
  )

print(p_free)

ggsave("pfas_free_fraction_vs_FBS.png", p_free, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot 2: Stacked partitioning (all phases) faceted by FBS level
# -- shows where the "lost" free fraction goes (e.g., into xbsa)
# as FBS increases, which is the mechanistic explanation for
# the reviewer comment about lipid/protein binding
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
cfree_long <- cfree_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(xplastic, xbsa, xslip, xcells, eta_free),
    names_to = "Phase",
    values_to = "Fraction"
  )

p_stack <- ggplot(cfree_long,
                   aes(x = Chemical, y = Fraction, fill = Phase)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ FBS_label, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
  labs(
    y = "Fraction in each phase",
    x = "Chemical",
    fill = "Phase",
    title = "PFAS partitioning across in vitro compartments at varying FBS levels"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
  )

print(p_stack)

ggsave("pfas_partitioning_by_FBS.png", p_stack, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```
